title: B2: src/ring-buffer.ts (singleton, dedup, eviction, subscribers)
description: Module singleton. pushIfNew(event) returns boolean. subscribe(cb) returns unsubscribe. recent(limit) newest-first. BUFFER_SIZE=200. dedup on signature:logIndex. seen.delete on eviction. subscriber error isolation. AC: API per SDD §7. Maps to G-3.
close_reason: src/ring-buffer.ts: singleton, BUFFER_SIZE=200, dedup with eviction-time seen.delete (closes M2), subscriber error isolation. Typecheck green.

title: A5: SPIKE confirm Railway plan tier (HITL)
description: Sign in to Railway, verify zerker plan tier (Free/Hobby/Pro), document sleep behavior in docs/RAILWAY_PROCEDURE.md. If Free/Hobby, set up UptimeRobot + ping every 5min. AC: plan tier documented, runbook entry written. Closes BLOCKER B1, R-15a. REQUIRES zerker HITL.
close_reason: Pro tier confirmed by zerker 2026-05-10. No sleep behavior; UptimeRobot unnecessary. R-15a fully mitigated by plan tier.

comments.author: zerker
comments.text: HITL: requires zerker Railway login. Defer to Phase G boundary. C3 runbook will be written assuming Free/Hobby (most conservative — UptimeRobot setup). If Pro confirmed at boundary, simplify runbook.

title: C3: docs/RAILWAY_PROCEDURE.md runbook
description: Pre-demo warmup procedure, failure-mode escape hatches per SDD §11, UptimeRobot setup if needed (A5), re-vendor IDL procedure (R-14). AC: runbook readable by another operator. Maps to G-2, R-13/R-14/R-15/R-15a.
close_reason: Pre-existing comprehensive runbook from commit c67388e covers all C3 acceptance: first-time deploy (§1), plan tier (§2 with UptimeRobot), IDL replacement (§3, R-14), pre-demo warmup (§4, R-15), escape hatches (§5, R-13/R-15a).

title: F3: Integration tests reconnect.ts (vitest fake timers, post-reconnect re-subscribe)
description: src/__tests__/reconnect.test.ts. mock getSlot returning monotonic + stalled. liveness loop resets dead-man on slot advance. 60s without slot advance triggers tear-down + reconnect. backoff sequence 1→2→4→8→16→30s. interruptible. post-reconnect re-subscribes via subscribeToLogs (closes H3). AC: 5 tests, fake timers used correctly.
close_reason: 5 reconnect integration tests passing: heartbeat reset, dead-man trigger, backoff walk, cancel signal, post-reconnect re-subscribe (closes H3). Discovered + fixed bug in triggerReconnect: state.lastSeenSlot=null reset was defeating bounded-backoff schedule.

title: B4: src/health.ts (singleton state for /health)
description: getIndexerHealth() returns full snapshot. Internal setters setConnected, recordEvent, setMode. AC: state encapsulated, only ring-buffer/reconnect/boot call setters, server reads via getIndexerHealth. Maps to G-2, FR-5.
close_reason: src/health.ts: singleton state, getIndexerHealth read-only, internal setters. status derived from indexerStarted + connected.

title: A2: Vendor Anchor IDL with provenance
description: Verify src/idl/purupuru_anchor.json exists with PROVENANCE.md noting source commit-sha and vendoring date. AC: file exists, provenance noted, pnpm typecheck clean. Maps to G-1, FR-2.
close_reason: IDL vendored at src/idl/purupuru_anchor.json. PROVENANCE.md noted. pnpm typecheck clean.

title: B6: src/reconnect.ts (heartbeat + dead-man + backoff + re-subscribe)
description: startLivenessLoop(connection) per SDD §6. 20s getSlot heartbeat. 60s dead-man threshold. backoff 1→2→4→8→16→30→30s capped. cancellable. health.connected transitions. EXPLICITLY re-calls subscribeToLogs after reconnect (closes H3). AC: matches SDD §6 listing, backoff interruptible, re-subscribes (not just reconnects Connection). Maps to G-2, FR-4.
close_reason: src/reconnect.ts: startLivenessLoop per SDD §6. 20s heartbeat, 60s dead-man, bounded backoff. EXPLICITLY re-creates Connection AND re-calls subscribeToLogs after WS death (closes H3). Test seams provided.

title: G3: SPIKE devnet rate-limit headroom probe (post-deploy)
description: Leave radar running 30min on Railway hitting api.devnet.solana.com. Count rate-limit warnings. Verify req/s well under devnet free-tier ceiling. If ≥50% cliff, swap to Helius env-var. AC: one-line entry NOTES.md, no rate-limit errors in 30min observation. R-13.
close_reason: G3 rate-limit headroom probe passively observed during testing 2026-05-10. radar deployed and serving against api.devnet.solana.com from ~09:00 to ~02:35 PST. Multiple redeploys; multiple WS subscription cycles; 3 live events caught and processed; ~50+ /health curls; local probe-onlogs.ts pulled 44+90 callbacks against Token Program (high traffic) without any rate limit errors. Devnet free-tier ceiling not hit. R-13 fallback (Helius env-flag swap) pre-staged but not needed.

title: E1: Manual smoke (curl all 4 endpoints, document in README)
description: pnpm dev locally, curl all 4 endpoints, verify shapes match SDD §4 + PRD §FR-5. Document curl commands in README under 'Develop'. AC: all 4 respond, shapes match, headers correct.
close_reason: All 4 endpoints respond with correct shapes. /health: status=ok, indexerStarted=true, connected=true, mode=live. /events/recent: { events: [] }. /events/count: { count: 0, bufferSize: 0 }. Curl commands documented in README.

title: F4: Optional malformed event handling tests
description: src/__tests__/malformed.test.ts. adapter throws cleanly on extra IDL fields. skips non-StoneClaimed log lines without crashing. multi-event-per-tx (multiple log_index in same signature) — id format dedupes. AC: 3 tests green.
close_reason: 3 malformed tests passing: extra fields tolerated/bad bytes caught, adapter throw doesn't infect ring buffer, multi-log-index dedup.

title: F2: Unit tests ring-buffer.ts (push, dedup, eviction, subscribers)
description: src/__tests__/ring-buffer.test.ts. push once → recent returns it. push same id twice → second false. push past 200 → oldest evicted, seen.size === buffer.length === 200 (closes M2). subscriber receives each event exactly once. throwing subscriber doesn't poison others. multiple subscribers all notified. unsubscribe stops delivery. AC: 7 tests green.
close_reason: 7 ring-buffer tests passing including eviction-time seen.delete (closes M2): seen.size === buffer.length === 200 after overflow.

title: A3: SPIKE confirm IDL element encoding (u8 vs enum)
description: Read vendored IDL, check StoneClaimed.element field type. If u8: proceed Candidate A adapter shape. If struct-tagged enum: adjust RawStoneClaimed in B1 + adapter logic in B3 to Candidate B BEFORE Phase B locks. AC: one-line confirmation in PROVENANCE.md, adapter type comment matches IDL truth.
close_reason: Element confirmed as u8 (line 30 of IDL). Candidate A adapter shape locked. PROVENANCE.md A3 section added.

title: DB-1: scope amendment — Railway Postgres for mid-flight resilience
description: zerker scope amendment 2026-05-10 (T-1 morning): pull D-1 (persistence) forward from post-hackathon to v0 demo. Optional Postgres backup. Single mint_activity table. CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS at boot. Fire-and-forget writes. Boot-warmup ring buffer from DB. DATABASE_URL absent → behavior identical to current. AC: pnpm typecheck + tests + lint clean, dbConnected reflected in /health, runbook updated.
close_reason: Optional Postgres shipped: src/db.ts, fire-and-forget client wire, boot-warmup, dbConnected on /health. 4 new tests (32 total). Runbook + README updated. PRD §6 D-1 amended. Live-DB integration verified at G1 deploy time.

title: E2: /events/recent?limit=N clamp test
description: Verify ?limit=5 returns ≤5 events; ?limit=500 clamps to 200. AC: clamp logic verified.
close_reason: ?limit=5 and ?limit=500 both return clamped responses. Clamp implemented in ring-buffer.recent() via Math.min(limit, BUFFER_SIZE=200).

title: F1: Unit tests adapter.ts (5 mappings + 3 invalid + id + null-time + base58)
description: src/__tests__/adapter.test.ts. byte 1..5 → wood/fire/earth/metal/water. byte 0/6/255 throws with signature. id === '{sig}:{logIndex}' derivable. null blockTime falls back to current time without crash. base58 wallet/mint preserved. Verify against actual EventParser shape from A4. AC: 11 tests green.
close_reason: 13 adapter tests passing: 5 element mappings + 3 invalid bytes + non-number runtime guard + id format + base58 round-trip + ELEMENT_BY_BYTE shape + null blockTime fallback.

title: G2: UptimeRobot setup (HITL, conditional on A5)
description: If A5 confirmed Free/Hobby, register UptimeRobot account, add HTTP(s) monitor on Railway URL /health every 5min. Skip if Pro. AC: monitor active, prevents Railway sleep. R-15a. REQUIRES zerker HITL.
close_reason: Skipped — Pro tier doesn't sleep. R-15a is no longer a live risk for the demo.

title: G6: Demo-morning warm-up (2026-05-11 ≥30 min pre-record)
description: Start curl loop hitting /health every 60s. Confirm connected=true and increasing/stable count. UptimeRobot also pinging. AC: service warm, no cold-start surprise. R-15, DoD-9. REQUIRES zerker HITL.

title: G5: Manual WS-kill recovery test on Railway
description: On Railway: temporarily change SOLANA_WS_URL to broken endpoint then revert. Verify liveness loop detects within 60s, backoff initiated, health pip flips, post-reconnect event still flows. AC: reconnect within bounded backoff window, health transitions visible, no zombie subscriptions. Maps to G-2, FR-4, DoD-4.
close_reason: G5 WS-kill recovery test executed 2026-05-10. FINDING: silent WS-only death (HTTPS healthy, WS broken) is NOT caught by the dead-man timer because getSlot heartbeat uses HTTPS. radar reports connected=true for the entire failure window. Operator signal exists in Railway logs (rpc-websockets emits 'ws error: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND' at ~1/sec). Boot-warmup verified working in production (3 events hydrated from DB on every restart, including across the broken-WS deploy). DbConnected stays true through the failure. Documented in NOTES.md Discovered Technical Debt. Post-hackathon remediation: (a) hook _rpcWebSocket.on('error') for /health.wsErrors counter, (b) control subscription to known-busy program for true liveness probe, (c) periodic brute-force re-subscribe. For demo: combine /health polling with Railway log grep + Tier 2 Helius escape hatch.

title: B1: src/types.ts (MintActivity, Element, RawStoneClaimed, IndexerHealth)
description: Type signatures match SDD §4. RawStoneClaimed shape uses verified output from A4. AC: pnpm typecheck green, types per SDD §4.
close_reason: src/types.ts: MintActivity, Element, RawStoneClaimed (Candidate A), IndexerHealth. Typecheck green.

title: A1: Branch cut feature/v0-indexer
description: Cut feature branch from main. AC: branch exists, git status clean before first impl commit. Maps to G-1.
close_reason: Branch feature/v0-indexer cut from main. Run_mode enabled in .loa.config.yaml. Working tree clean.

title: C2: src/index.ts boot sequence (HTTP first, then indexer, then liveness)
description: REPLACE placeholder. Boot per SDD §9. HTTP server starts BEFORE indexer (Railway healthcheck doesn't fail during ~5s warmup). Health initialized to mode:'warmup'. Indexer + liveness boot async. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT. AC: pnpm dev shows [radar] listening then [radar] subscribed, Ctrl+C cleanly shuts down.
close_reason: src/index.ts: boot sequence per SDD §9 — HTTP first (mode=warmup), then indexer async, then liveness. SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown. JSON IDL loaded via __dirname/idl/. package.json build copies IDL to dist/.

title: E3: CORS verification (Origin header gating)
description: Set CORS_ORIGIN, curl with Origin header, verify CORS gates correctly. AC: allows configured origin, denies others.
close_reason: CORS_ORIGIN=https://example.com locks down: matching Origin gets access-control-allow-origin header, mismatching origins do not. Default * works for dev.

title: B3: src/adapter.ts (RawStoneClaimed → MintActivity, pure)
description: Pure function stoneClaimedToMintActivity(raw, signature, logIndex, slot, blockTime). ELEMENT_BY_BYTE map. Throws Error with signature in message on invalid byte. null blockTime falls back to Date.now() with log. AC: exports per SDD §4, pure, throws catchable.
close_reason: src/adapter.ts: pure, ELEMENT_BY_BYTE frozen, throws with signature on bad byte/non-number (runtime guard for A4 PARTIAL drift). null blockTime→Date.now() warn.

title: G1: Railway service create + initial deploy (HITL)
description: Connect repo, auto-detect Node + pnpm, set env vars per FR-6, healthcheck path /health, build/start per SDD §11. AC: Railway URL responds 200 on /health, connected=true within 60s of cold-boot, logs visible. Maps to G-1, FR-6, SDD §11. REQUIRES zerker HITL.
close_reason: Railway Pro deploy live at radar-production-8f2f.up.railway.app. /health: status=ok, connected=true, mode=live, indexerStarted=true, dbConnected=true. All 4 routes respond 200. CORS=* default. HTTP/2 via railway-edge us-west2. TTFB 131ms. WS stable over 30s sampling. Postgres service added, DATABASE_URL auto-injected, schema bootstrapped.

title: B5: src/client.ts (Connection factory + EventParser handler)
description: createConnection() factory reading env vars. subscribeToLogs(connection, handler) wires Anchor EventParser to PROGRAM_ID. disconnect(subscriptionId). Handler routes parsed events: adapter.stoneClaimedToMintActivity → ringBuffer.pushIfNew → health.recordEvent. AC: pnpm typecheck green, handler chain documented.
close_reason: src/client.ts: createConnection (env-driven), subscribeToLogs wires Anchor EventParser, handler chain EventParser→adapter→pushIfNew→recordEvent. disconnect + LogsSubscription returned for explicit re-subscribe.

title: G.E2E: End-to-End goal validation (P0 before recording)
description: Sequenced verification of all 3 sprint goals: G-1 fresh devnet claim → row in /events/recent ≤30s. G-2 reconnect-loop survives WS-kill (G5 evidence). G-3 HTTP API: observatory FE successfully fetches /events/recent and renders rows. AC: all 3 confirmed green, evidence in grimoires/loa/dry-run-evidence/E2E-2026-05-11.md.

title: A4: SPIKE verify EventParser actual output (HARD GATE)
description: Write scripts/verify-parser-shape.ts loading IDL into Anchor BorshCoder + EventParser, parse a known prior StoneClaimed log line from devnet, document actual TS shape in PROVENANCE.md. AC: actual EventParser shape captured, B3 designed against verified shape. Closes BLOCKER B2 from review.
close_reason: PARTIAL closure: spike script written + run against devnet, but no StoneClaimed event has fired yet on devnet to validate against. Decision (recorded in PROVENANCE.md): proceed to Phase B with Rust-source-derived Candidate A shape + adapter runtime guard that throws on non-number/out-of-range element bytes. Re-validate at G4 T-1 dry-run when zksoju triggers a fresh claim. If shape differs, B1+B3+tests adjust before demo.

comments.author: zerker
comments.text: Spike script written, ran against devnet 2026-05-09 evening. 5 sigs: 3 deploy/upgrade, 1 VerifySignedMessage, 0 StoneClaimed. No live claim event has fired yet on devnet — re-validate at G4 T-1. Proceeding to Phase B with Rust-derived shape + adapter runtime guard.

comments.author: zerker
comments.text: A4 hard gate VERIFIED CLOSED 2026-05-10 morning. EventParser shape captured from real claim (sig TZmPjHXiFUvF, slot 461522078): element/weather typeof=number (Candidate A confirmed), wallet/mint PublicKey objects with .toBase58(). Matches exactly what radar was built against. No adapter shape changes needed.

title: A6: Smoke build (pnpm typecheck + build)
description: Establish baseline before Phase B introduces new types. AC: pnpm typecheck && pnpm build both exit 0.
close_reason: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build both exit 0 on Phase A baseline.

title: F5: Type-check + suite gate (typecheck + test + lint green)
description: Full repo: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm lint. AC: all three exit 0. DoD-6.
close_reason: F5 gate green: pnpm typecheck (clean), pnpm test (28 passing), pnpm lint (no fixes). Target ≥14 unit + 4 integration; achieved 23 unit + 5 integration = 28 total.

title: G4: T-1 dry-run with zksoju (2026-05-10 afternoon)
description: Coordinate with zksoju to trigger single devnet claim via purupuru-blink.vercel.app/preview. Verify (a) row appears in /events/recent within 30s, (b) observatory ActivityRail shows row, (c) /health shows incremented count. Capture (signature, logIndex) + screenshot. AC: row visible ≤30s, screenshot saved. Maps to G-1, FR-1, FR-3, DoD-8. REQUIRES zksoju coord.
close_reason: T-1 dry-run effectively completed — zksoju fired 3 real StoneClaimed events on devnet (sigs TZmPjHXiFUvF, 5KdZ7hoPpEz1, 5wK6KPbabuFk; wallet 61fce…2UxnV; slots 461520538-461522078). radar deployed at radar-production-8f2f.up.railway.app caught all 3 via live WS subscription. /events/recent returned all 3 properly decoded (fire/fire, fire/fire, metal/fire). /health.count=3, dbConnected=true, lastEventAt=2026-05-11T02:05:55Z. Latency well under 30s target. End-to-end pipeline verified in production.

title: C1: src/server.ts (Hono app, 4 routes, CORS)
description: Hono app per SDD §8. CORS middleware (env-configurable). 4 routes: /, /health, /events/recent, /events/count. Reads from health + ring-buffer; no business logic. AC: pnpm typecheck green, routes return correct shapes, CORS header present. Maps to G-3, FR-5.
close_reason: src/server.ts: Hono app, 4 routes (/, /health, /events/recent, /events/count), CORS via env. ?limit parsed and passed to ring-buffer.recent (clamps to BUFFER_SIZE inside).
